description: Th' world's first workin' PlayStation Vita emulator, arrr!
vita3k_description: Vita3K be th' first ever open-source PlayStation Vita emulator fer Windows, Linux, macOS, an' Android, matey!
legal_notice: Avast! This emulator ain't fer breakin' the law, savvy?
get_in_touch: Send a message in a bottle!
get_in_touch_description: Holler at the crew or other pirates whenever ye wish. Discord's our tavern o' choice, but pick yer poison!
the_emulator_can_run_some_commercial_gam: This emulator can run some treasured games!
several_homebrew_games_are_also_supporte: A fair share o' homebrew games be supported too. Check the homebrew compatibilARRRty list fer more loot.
trademarks_notice: PlayStation an' PS Vita be marks o' the mighty Sony, arrr!
emulator_notice: This emulator ain't got nothin' to do with Sony, nor be it made from their secret maps, arrr!
contribute_ways: Ye can pitch in to the emulator like so, matey:
ko_fi_page: If ye want to toss some gold our way, the project has a Ko-fi Page.
file_issues_or_make_pull_requests: Report yer troubles or send us yer code on our GitHub repository.
whats_new: What be new on the high seas?
blog_description: We got a captain's log where we scribble tales o' our latest adventures. Come see what be new!
nothing: Nothin', not even a parrot squawk
homebrew_that_crash_the_emulator: Homebrew that send the emulator to Davy Jones' locker.
homebrew_that_work_with_bugs_or_glitches: Homebrew that work, but be haunted by bugs an' curses.
homebrew_that_work_but_have_very_poor_fr: Homebrew that work, but move slower than a sea turtle.
nothing_desc: Games that don't boot at all, not even a black flag with a FPS counter.
bootable_desc: Games that boot but only show a black flag with a FPS counter, arrr!
intro_desc: Games that show somethin' on the screen but never reach the galley table (menu).
menu_desc: Games that reach the galley table but can't set sail (ingame).
ingame_minus_desc: Games that go ingame but hit a reef early on, stoppin' progress.
ingame_plus_desc: Games that go far ingame but be cursed with glitches or run like a leaky ship.
playable_desc: Games that can be played from start to finish, smooth sailin' and no mutinies!
emulator_undergoing_changes:
The emulator be gettin' a mighty overhaul below deck, improvin' compatibilARRRty with games.
This list be out o' date, so all ye interested pirates should re-test yer games with the latest builds every moon or so, and update the logbook!
get_started: Hoist the anchor and play yer favorite PSVita booty!
hardware_requirements_desc: How well the emulator sails depends on yer ship (hardware). If yer vessel barely meets the minimum, expect rough seas. For smooth sailin', make sure yer ship be up to snuff, as most reports come from sturdy vessels.
gpu_that_supports_opengl_version: A GPU that speaks OpenGL {version}, arrr!
minimum_of_4gb_of_ram: At least 4GB o' RAM in yer hold
gpu_that_supports_vulkan: A GPU that knows Vulkan sorcery
gpu_that_supports_shader_interlock: A GPU that can wrangle shader interlock
x86_64_cpu_with_avx: x86_64 CPU with the AVX black magic
minimum_of_8gb_of_ram: 8GB o' RAM or more, ye greedy pirate
microsoft_redistributable_desc: If Vita3K be complainin' about VCRUNTME140_1.dll was not found, fetch and install the Visual C++ 2015-2022 Redistributable from the royal treasury.
operating_system_desc: Ye need a 64-bit system to set sail with Vita3K.
firmware_desc: Some games need the system modules aboard for Vita3K to work its magic. Install the PS Vita firmware through Vita3K, matey.
firmware_download: Ye can plunder the firmware from the official PlayStation website.
font_firmware_desc: There be an extra package with the system fonts. Download it straight from the PlayStation servers, arrr!
could_not_get_url: But we couldn't chart the course to the URL, so ye must find it yerself. Or ask in the discord tavern with the -firmware command.
install_both_firmware_packages: Install both firmware packages usin' the File > Install Firmware menu.
managing_modules_desc: Manage yer system modules in Configuration > Settings > Core. We recommend Modules Mode > Automatic. If some modules be mutinous, try tossin' 'em overboard.
